titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Records of Athletics Programs at New York University RG.41
titlestmt.author: Claire Wolford. Additional processing by Elizabeth Alleva, Stacey Flatt, and Lauren Stark.

did.unittitle: Records of Athletics Programs at New York University

physdesc.extent: in 168 manuscript boxes, 7 half manuscript boxes, 1 tall manuscript box, 31 record cartons, 11 custom oversize boxes, 2 small flat boxes, 15 oversize flat boxes, 2 LP boxes, 8 cassette boxes, 7 CD boxes, 1 card box, 2 oversize folders, 1 folder in a shared manuscript box, and physical digital media distributed among 1 shared CD box and 3 shared card boxes

did.abstract: This collection contains the records of the New York University Athletics Department dating from 1872-2016. These records include materials on intercollegiate and intramural sports which typically includes rosters, statistics, press releases, schedules, and team and game photographs. Digital and analog audio and video recordings of interviews, games, and season highlight reels are also in this collection. Also included are general departmental files and materials on Coles Sports and Recreation Center.
did.physloc: Materials are stored offsite and advance notice is required for use. Please request materials at least two business days prior to your research visit to coordinate access.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Administrative records and unpublished reports of New York University are closed for a period of 20 years from the date of their creation. Access to files spanning multiple years will be opened to researchers based on the date of the most recent materials. Board of Trustees records are closed for 35 years from the date of creation. Materials related to personnel, grievances, job and fellowship searches and applications, and all files that fall under the federal Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) are permanently restricted. Additional restrictions may apply to other materials in this collection. For questions regarding specific restrictions, please contact the University Archives.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred by the Athletics Department to the archives in numerous accessions between 1992-2023. The accession numbers associated with these records are 92.033, 06.029, 12.013, 13.039, 15.016, 15.043, 16.016, 2016.004, 2016.042, 2018.022, 2019.033, 2020.034, and 2023.048.

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p:
Duplicate pieces of the Bobcat costume were deaccessioned in February 2019.
Sixteen cartons of paper were appraised out of the collection during processing, including duplicates and items considered out of scope of the collection (e.g., individual game scores, non-Athletics Department NYU publications, contracts, checkbooks, insurance and other vendor information, incident and injury reports, medical records, consent forms, and lawsuit materials). For both print and digital images, the following were removed: unlabeled (i.e., unnamed) headshots of individual athletes, staff, and coaches. Any NYU Photo Bureau images were also removed from the collection.

Approximately 16-20 cartons of audiovisual material and computer disks were also removed from the collection during the processing of new material into the collection in 2025-2026. Imaged born-digital carriers with a large number of similar photographs from athletic and other events were sampled, retaining a representative 10% of the images, and deaccessioning the remainder. Off-air recordings, in both analog and digital formats, have been appraised out of the collection due to copyright limitations. These recordings include commercial musical recordings and any recordings from a copyrighted broadcast or television transmission. Additionally, the following recordings were removed from the collection: scrimmages, practices, scouting videos, logos, videos with extremely poor image quality, and any NYU-TV recordings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p:
The collection is organized into series that are based on the New York University Archives' record group classification system. Titles for the series are specific sports (e.g., Basketball, Tennis, and Golf) or divisions related to sports (e.g., Athletics Department Administrative Records). When appropriate, the series are further divided into subseries for men's and women's teams, followed by a subseries for combined men's and women's team materials. Sport materials are arranged chronologically by season, thereunder alphabetically within these series and subseries unless specificed otherwise.
The collection has been arranged into 34 series, 12 of which have been further arranged into subseries. The series and subseries arrangement is as follows:

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p:
Athletic competition began at New York University in 1873, when the first intercollegiate games in baseball and football were played. Formal intercollegiate competition did not begin until 1894, when NYU's move to the University Heights campus in the Bronx provided space for the athletics program to grow. The University erected a new gymnasium as well as football and baseball fields. By 1907 the University sponsored varsity baseball, football, basketball, tennis, and track teams.
Women's sports such as swimming, tennis, and basketball began as informal coed activities in the 1920s and later became varsity programs. A number of the University's students, coaches, and alumni have participated in the Olympics including Sam Jones, Howard and Ted Cann, Jose and Miguel De Capriles, Byron Dyce, and Carol Heiss.
The University's financial struggles forced the Athletics department to shut down both football and basketball in the 1970s as NYU left its Bronx campus. Athletics at NYU experienced a resurgence in the 1980s with the construction of the Jerome S. Coles Sports Center and the move from Division I to Division III competition. The basketball team was reactivated in 1983 and continues to play. As of 2026, NYU has varsity teams in basketball, cross country, fencing, golf, soccer, swimming and diving, tennis, track and field, volleyball, and wrestling. Baseball returned as a varsity sport in 2015 and softball was added as a result of NYU's completed merger with NYU-Poly (now New York University Tandon School of Engineering).

phystech.head: Audiovisual Access Policies and Procedures
phystech.p: Access to some audiovisual materials in this collection is available through digitized access copies. Researchers may view an item's original container, but the media themselves are not available for playback because of preservation concerns. Materials that have already been digitized are noted in the collection's finding aid and can be requested in our reading room.

Materials not yet digitized will need to have access copies made before they can be used. To request an access copy, or if you are unsure if an item has been digitized, please contact NYU Special Collections (special.collections@nyu.edu, 212-998-2596) with the collection name, collection number, and a description of the item(s) requested. A staff member will respond to you with further information.

phystech.head: Born-Digital Access Policies and Procedures
phystech.p: An access terminal for born-digital materials in the collection is available by appointment for reading room viewing and listening only. Researchers may view an item's original container and/or carrier, but the physical carriers themselves are not available for use because of preservation concerns. Advance notice is required for the use of computer records. Original physical digital media is restricted.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date; Records of Athletics Programs at NYU; RG 41; box number; folder number or item identifier; New York University Archives, New York University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p:
Decisions regarding arrangement, description, and physical interventions for this collection prior to 2019 are unknown. Duplicate pieces of the Bobcat Costume were deaccessioned in February 2019. In June 2019 memorabilia was rehoused by preservation and sent offsite. In September 2019 wrestling student-athlete registration cards (1985-1986) underwent basic mold cleaning.
In 2020, 3 boxes of unprocessed material were inspected by Preservation staff for mold; items showing no evidence of mold were removed from their original boxes and moved to another box in the collection. Materials with evidence of mold were sent to Polygon for cleaning in early 2020 and returned in September 2020.
In 2025-2026, all unprocessed accretions to this collection were processed. Materials were placed into acid-free legal boxes and folders, and oversized materials were rehoused in appropriately sized boxes. Materials in binders were removed from their enclosures, and some items were removed from non-archival sleeves. Duplicate publications, recordings, and documents, publications widely available online or in NYU's catalog holdings, and documents with sensitive personal information were removed. One box of film was transferred to Records of the Gallatin Division (RG 36). Two boxes from the Records of the Jerome S. Coles Sports and Recreation Center (RG 41.8) were transferred to this collection and intellectually incorporated into the arrangement. Seven cartons of NYU yearbooks were separated from the collection and transferred to the University Archives staff.
1174 CDs, DVDs, floppy disks, and zip disks were forensically imaged, analyzed, and arranged in Forensic Toolkit. Carriers with a large number of similar photographs from athletic and other events were digitally sampled, retaining 10%. New York University Libraries follow professional standards and best practices when imaging, ingesting, and processing born-digital material in order to maintain the integrity and authenticity of the content.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related materials include: Athletic Department Scrapbook (MC 78); the University Archives Photo Collection (PHOTO 0001); Historic Photographs and Drawings Collection (PHOTO 002); New York University Archives Audiovisual Collection (AV); and NYU Archives Subject Files (MC 308).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains records for various athletics programs and the Athletics Department at New York University. Team and individual sports material in this collection dates from 1872-2016 and typically includes born-digital and paper files of press clippings, press releases, statistics, rosters, schedules, and event programs. Photographs typically consist of digital and print images of teams and staff from each season, individual player headshots, game action shots, and awards ceremonies. Digital and analog audio and video recordings in this collection include interviews, games, and season highlight reels. This collection also contains season preview guides, also known as media guides, which include analysis and commentary from the previous season and highlight the upcoming season by providing team rosters, game schedules, and photographs of players and staff. Athletics Department and Jerome S. Coles Sports Center files include intramural sport schedules and brochures, press releases, correspondence, and files related to the Hall of Fame Ceremony.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Any rights (including copyright and related rights to publicity and privacy) held by New York University are maintained by New York University. Permission to publish or reproduce materials in this collection must be secured from the University Archives. Please contact NYU Special Collections, special.collections@nyu.edu, 212-998-2596.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Seven cartons of New York University yearbooks were separated from the collection and sent back to University Archives staff to be reviewed.

physdesc.extent: in 27 manuscript boxes, 1 half manuscript box, 18 record cartons, 6 oversize custom boxes, 8 cassette boxes, 1 card box, 1 small flat box, and 6 oversize flat boxes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials related to NYU's varsity sports as a whole. Dating from 1871-2014, materials include press, statistics, schedules, and rosters of all of the varsity athletic teams. A large portion of the series is dedicated to athletic award ceremonies, such as the Hall of Fame Induction and Student-Athlete Academic Achievement presentation. Additionally, there are files on women's varsity athletic awards, including a Women's Hall of Fame, which were celebrated separately through the mid-1990s. Awards' materials include event video recordings, invitations and programs, and press. Also included in this series are materials related to alumni clubs (such as the Letter Club, Varsity Club, and Violet Booster Club) that fundraise for athletics teams; these materials consist of meeting minutes, correspondence, and brochures. Publications related to athletics are included, such as yearbooks, general athletics brochures, and weekly newsletters. The costumes for NYU's mascots, the Bobcat and the Violet, are found in this series, as well as uniforms for unidentified sports. In addition, video recordings of multisport events and unidentified sporting events, along with photographs of coaches and athletes from unidentified sports, are included here.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the NYU men's and women's basketball teams. In addition to the press, statistics, and rosters typically found in most sport-specific series (which are described at the collection-level), this series contains handwritten scorebooks, uniforms, and game tickets and programs. Materials related to the women's 1996–1997 season, during which they won the Division III NCAA National Championship, include souvenir pennants, posters, photographs, game programs, and digital and analog audio and video recordings of interviews, games, and season highlight reels. Individual athlete files in this series range in date from 1932 to 1971 and typically contain brief biographical descriptions, photographs, and press material. This series also contains materials with combined information on the men's and women's teams including game programs, press information, as well as photographs, slides, and contact sheets.

physdesc.extent: in 28 manuscript boxes, 1 half manuscript box, 14 record cartons, 6 cassette boxes, 1 oversize box, 1 small flat box, and 5 oversize flat boxes

bioghist.head: History of NYU Men's Basketball
bioghist.p:
The men's basketball program began as an intra-school sport in 1897, progressing to varsity team competition in 1906. During the 1919-1920 season, the Violets participated in the National Championship Tournament where they became the National and World Champions. Howard Cann, who would go on to coach the men's team at NYU for 35 seasons, was voted the best player at that tournament.
By the 1970s, the University cut the basketball program for budgetary reasons. Following a gradual improvement in the school's financial standing, NYU reintroduced basketball in 1983 in Division III competition.

bioghist.head: History of NYU Women's Basketball
bioghist.p: The women's basketball program was initially considered a coed activity beginning in 1923, until it became a varsity sport in the 1930s. As of 2026, the team has won three Division III NCAA national championships (1997, 2024, 2025).

bioghist.head: History of NYU Baseball
bioghist.p:
NYU's baseball program was established in the 1870s as something akin to a club sport. The team was not very successful in its early years, however, with the varsity team losing all but one of 13 games in 1902.
The team experienced a resurgence in the 1920s under coach William McCarthy. Between 1922 and 1961, he led the NYU Violets to 476 victories and 258 losses. Among the outstanding players of the 1920s was George "Kiddo" Davis, the first NYU baseball starter to break into the major leagues.
During the University's financial struggles in the early 1970s, the baseball team was discontinued. Baseball was reinstated in 1996 as a Division II club sport, joining the National Club Baseball Association (NCBA). Varsity baseball returned to NYU in spring 2015 as a Division III sport when NYU-Poly (now NYU Tandon School of Engineering), which had its own team, officially became part of NYU.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials related to the NYU baseball team dating from 1872-2015. In addition to the press, statistics, and rosters typically found in most sports-specific series (which ware described at the collection-level), items include game tickets, a scrapbook created for the 1902 season, files on individual players, and meeting minutes from the Metropolitan Collegiate Baseball Conference, of which NYU was a member. Of note is a baseball jersey from approximately the 1950s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material related to the NYU men's and women's fencing team, dating from 1923 to 2015. In addition to the press, statistics, and rosters typically found in most sport-specific series (which are described at the collection-level), this series also includes team histories; all-time records lists from the 1920 to the 1960s; and individual athlete and coach files, which typically contain photographs, brief biographical material, and press releases and clippings. Materials related to the men's team's NCAA championship wins from the 1950s to the 1970s are also in this series, as well as photographs and press releases related to a visit in the early 1970s from former NYU team member Neil Diamond. This series also contains team equipment, including a saber, foil, and épée. In the 2000s, the season preview guides combined the men's and women's team information into one publication which is located in the combined subseries.

bioghist.head: History of NYU Football
bioghist.p: The NYU football team was founded in 1873 and was one of the first university-sponsored teams in the United States. NYU was integral to the regulation of college athletics in the early 20th century, helping to found the Intercollegiate Athletic Association of the United States, the precursor to the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA). In 1953 the team was disbanded. The football program made a short return in 1964 as a club sport, but eventually was discontinued in 1967.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains records related to the New York University football team dating from 1873 to 2011. In addition to the press, statistics, and rosters typically found in most sports-specific series (which are described at the collection-level), this series also contains team histories, game programs, tickets/ticket request forms, and documentation related to continuing the football program during the 1960s to 1970s. Individual player and coach files within this series contain bios, photographs, and press-related materials dating from 1916 to 1978.

In addition to the press, statistics, and rosters typically found in most sports-specific series (which are described at the collection-level), items such as a uniform top, warm-up set, and files on individual athletes are included. accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to Shared University Archives Box 159, Folder 1, is restricted until 2086.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of administrative materials created and collected by NYU's Athletics Department. Materials consist of annual reports (which provide season summaries for each sport), staff photographs and biographical information, correspondence, committee meeting minutes (mostly for early committees such as the Board of Advisers for Women's Athletics and the Undergraduate Athletic Board), enrollment statistics (for both varsity and intramural sports), and a video recording of a student-athlete public service announcement. A large portion of the series relates to the construction of the Jerome S. Coles Sports Center in 1981; related materials include architectural blueprints and drawings, photographs, and correspondence regarding community engagement. Also included are chronological files of Albert B. Nixon, the graduate manager of athletics from 1922-1940s, and Esther Foley, the director of the women's athletic program from 1951-1971.

physdesc.extent: in 5 manuscript boxes, 2 half manuscript boxes, and 2 record cartons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to the Jerome S. Coles Sports Center, which opened in 1981 and closed in 2016. The Center was the main location for NYU's department of athletics and intramural sports. Materials in this series consist of club and class schedules and brochures, club correspondence and photographs, general correspondence, and information on class and club memberships. Also included are brochures and schedules from other NYU athletic facilities that existed before the construction of the Center and from other facilities, mainly Chelsea Piers, which provided spaces for clubs and classes in the mid-1990s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials from the University Athletic Association, an intercollegiate athletic conference that competes in the National Collegiate Athletic Association Division III with NYU as a member. Materials, dating from 1986-1995, include press releases and mailings which list conference standings, athletes of the week, and other statistics and records. Much of this information is also included in each of the individual sport series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is comprised of materials related to NYU Olympic athletes from 1904-1992. Materials consist of team rosters, biographies of these athletes, and press. Also included are a booklet, scrapbook, and correspondence from a 1988 exhibition on NYU athletes who have competed in the Olympics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to the NYU men's and women's swim teams dating from 1923-2015. In addition to the press, statistics, and roster typically found in most sports-specific series (which are described the collection-level), items of note consist of historical lists of coaches, scorebooks, event posters, and video recordings from UAA Championships.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes material for the NYU wrestling team dating from 1934 to 2012. In addition to the press, statistics, and rosters typically found in most sports-specific series (which are described at the collection-level), this series also contains a list of team captains from 1934 to 1987, athlete awards lists, newsletters, documentation of the Bulgarian National Wrestling Team visit to NYU in 1987, and uniform pieces (singlet and warm-up top).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials related to NYU's softball team, which started as a club sport and became a varsity sport in the fall of 2014. Materials, dating from 1993-2015, include press, rosters, statistics, and photographs.

physdesc.extent: in 1 manuscript box and 1 half manuscript box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material related to the NYU men's and women's bowling teams dating from the 1940s to the 1970s. Materials include a circa 1970s photograph of the men's team, and women's team roster, schedules, photographs, and correspondence.

physdesc.extent: in 1 manuscript box and 1 half manuscript box
in 1 manuscript box and 1 half manuscript box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the men's and women's rifle teams, dated from the 1920s-late 1960s, including photographs, statistics, press, and award lists.

physdesc.extent: in 1 manuscript box and 1 half manuscript box
in 1 manuscript box and 1 half manuscript box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to the women's field hockey team, which was a varsity sport in the 1920s-1940s. Materials, dating from 1932-1947, consist of photographs, a scorebook, and game results.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is comprised of materials related to NYU's lacrosse team, dating from 1887-2002, including photographs, results, and press. Of note is documentation of NYU fielding the first national college lacrosse team in 1887.

did.unittitle: History of NYU's First College Lacrosse Team

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials related to the NYU men's water polo team, dating from 1982-1986, including photographs, schedules, and a flyer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of a 1999 unidentified video recording related to tae bo, which was likely a class offered by NYU's Campus Recreation division.

physdesc.extent: in 1 manuscript box, 1 oversize flat box, and 1 cassette box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the NYU coed rowing club. Dating from 1968-2011, materials include photographs, press, results, and video footage from 1999.

physdesc.extent: in 1 manuscript box, 1 oversize box, and 1 cassette box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material related to the NYU dance team dating from 1986 to 2012 and includes photographs, press, roster, team information press packet, try-out information, and a performance uniform.

physdesc.extent: in 1 manuscript box, 1 oversize flat box, and 1 cassette box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material related to the NYU cheerleading team dating from 1943 to 2013. It includes correspondence related to the establishment of the squad in the 1940s and uniforms (top, skirt, warm-up pants, and men's warm-up pants).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the NYU men's ice hockey club, which currently competes in the American Collegiate Hockey Association. Materials date from 1991-2013 and include press, programs, and statistics. In addition, there is a uniform top and multiple video recordings, including one of a game against the Polish Junior National Team.

scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to NYU's cricket league, dating from 1961-1963, consisting of correspondence and a schedule.

scopecontent.p: This series is comprised of materials related to NYU's coed kendo club. Dating from 2007-2012, materials include correspondence, schedules, results, and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the coed table tennis team, which was founded at NYU in 2006. Materials from 2010-2012 include correspondence, budget proposals, and a flyer.

scopecontent.p: This series consists of a circa 1929 photograph related to boxing at NYU, which was an official sport in the 1920s-1930s.
